writePrerequisites.description: akita-accept must consume the persisted write report above and stop when it is missing instead of guessing which generated artifacts are eligible for live acceptance.

requiredMachineState.description: Canonical local accept trace. It must record the explicit accept request, the validation findings, each copied live destination, and every blocker or refusal in one durable JSON file.

validationPolicy.description: akita-accept must validate the selected generated artifacts against manifest-listed capability truth and the lineage recorded by akita-write before it copies anything into live repo paths.

acceptancePolicy.description: akita-accept must treat write-report as the source-of-truth artifact bundle, require explicit human-chosen live destinations, validate before copy, copy instead of move, and refuse overwrites or pack-managed destinations.

stopConditions.description: The write report is missing, so accept cannot know which generated artifact bundle is eligible.
A manifest-listed capability bundle asset is missing, so accept must stop instead of guessing validation rules.
The request names an artifact id that is not present in the current write report.
Accept requires an explicit repo-relative destination for each selected artifact.
The generated source file recorded in write-report is missing on disk.
The emitted source path falls outside the contract-defined generated namespace.
The requested destination is not a safe repo-relative path.
Accept must not publish into `.oma/` or `.opencode/`.
The destination already exists, so accept must stop instead of overwriting it.
Unsupported steps or assertions must block accept explicitly with clear findings.
Any construct that cannot be grounded in manifest-listed bundle truth must block accept explicitly.
Generated artifacts that diverge from the lineage recorded by akita-write must block accept explicitly.

summaryState.description: Human-readable accept summary only. Canonical machine state stays in the JSON file above.
